subject: DESIGN: Firestore organizer collection for AI-discovered → TT.com apply flow
body:
Fulton,

Ybotman directive — new architecture for organizer outreach pipeline.

## The Problem

Booker (fb-conditioner) has 315 organizers with FB Messenger links, 14 with emails, 46 with websites. These are people who host tango events on Facebook. We want to contact them and convert them into TT.com organizers who manage their own events.

## Proposed Flow

1. Booker writes discovered organizer records to Firestore collection `discoveredOrganizers`
2. Campaign system (AIDI/manual) contacts organizers via FB Messenger
3. Message includes a personalized apply link: tt.com/apply?orgToken=XXX
4. TT.com apply page reads Firestore to PREFILL the organizer application:
   - Name (from FB)
   - City/State (from their events)
   - FB page URL (verified)
   - Website (if found)
   - Email (if found)
   - Their discovered events (list)
5. Organizer reviews, completes, submits
6. Approved → becomes real organizer in MongoDB
7. Their AI-discovered events transition to organizer-managed events

## Proposed Firestore Schema: discoveredOrganizers

```
{
  id: string,                    // auto-generated
  name: string,                  // from FB host extraction
  fbProfileUrl: string,          // messageable link
  fbMessengerId: string | null,  // if available
  email: string | null,          // from FB page scrape
  website: string | null,        // from FB page scrape
  city: string | null,           // from their events
  state: string | null,
  country: string,
  hostType: string,              // INDIVIDUAL, PAGE, ORGANIZATION
  eventCount: number,            // how many events they host
  sampleEventTitles: string[],   // their top events (for apply prefill)
  
  // Campaign tracking
  outreachStatus: string,        // not_contacted, contacted, responded, applied, approved, declined
  contactedAt: timestamp | null,
  contactedVia: string | null,   // 'fb_messenger', 'email'
  responseAt: timestamp | null,
  orgToken: string | null,       // for personalized apply link
  
  // Apply tracking  
  appliedAt: timestamp | null,
  mongoOrgId: string | null,     // after approval, link to MongoDB organizer
  
  // Metadata
  discoveredAt: timestamp,
  lastSeenAt: timestamp,
  source: string,                // 'fb-conditioner', 'harvester'
}
```

## Questions for you

1. Does the TT.com apply flow already read from Firestore? Can we add a prefill path?
2. Is the orgToken link generation already built? (I saw it referenced in your recent commits)
3. What fields does the current apply form need? I want to match what Booker captures.
4. Any concerns about campaign writing directly to Firestore (no API layer)?

## What Booker will build

- `scripts/export-to-firestore.ts` — reads organizers from SQLite, writes to Firestore `discoveredOrganizers` collection
- Runs after each enrichment cycle
- Idempotent (upsert by fbProfileUrl)

## What Fulton needs to build

- Apply page reads `discoveredOrganizers` when orgToken is present
- Prefills form fields from Firestore record
- On approval, creates MongoDB organizer + updates Firestore status

— Booker
